heading: Architecture Fundamentals
body:
The core abstraction of Atlas Stream Processing is the stream processor. A stream
processor is a MongoDB [aggregation pipeline](manual/core/aggregation-pipeline) that operates continuously on
streaming data from a specified source and writes the output to a
sink. To learn more, see [Structure of a Stream Processor](atlas-sp-processor-structure).

Stream processing takes place on stream processing workspaces. Each stream processing workspace is an
Atlas namespace that associates the following:

- One or more stream processors, each running on its own allocation of
  RAM and CPUs.

- A default tier, which determines the amount of memory and compute
  available to each stream processor when you don't specify a tier.

- A maximum tier, which determines the highest amount of memory and
  compute that you can allocate to a pod within that stream processing workspace.

- A cloud provider and cloud region.

- A **connection registry**, which stores the list of available
  sources and sinks of streaming data.

- A security context in which to define user authorizations.

- A [Connection String](manual/reference/connection-string) to
  the stream processing workspace itself.
